zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron Simon de Pierrepont於大約1255年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特時，他的父親Lord Henry de Pierrepont25歲，母親Annora Manvers22歲。他於大約1300年與Mary在英國諾廷加姆爾霍爾貝可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世在英國諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特。

nameMeanings.name: Pierrepont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Baron Simon de Pierrepont was born about 1255, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Lord Henry de Pierrepont, was 25 and his mother, Annora Manvers, was 22. He married Mary about 1300, in Holbeck, Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron Simon de Pierrepont est né environ 1255, à Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Lord Henry de Pierrepont, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Annora Manvers, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Mary environ 1300, à Holbeck, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé à Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Baron Simon de Pierrepontが約1255年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアホールメ ピエレプオントで生まれた時，父Lord Henry de Pierrepontは25歳，母Annora Manversは22歳であった。約1300年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアホールベックでMaryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアホールメ ピエレプオントで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron Simon de Pierrepont(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Henry de Pierrepont(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Annora Manvers(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1300년 영국 노틴그함실르 홀벡에서 Mary(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron Simon de Pierrepont родился около 1255 г. в местности холм пьиррепонт, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Lord Henry de Pierrepont было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Annora Manvers, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary около 1300 г. в местности холбекк, ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в местности холм пьиррепонт, ноттингхамшир, Англия.
